ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bento class; no specific unagi-teriyaki-chicken bento subclass exists. Mapped to closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers bento box meals broadly; no specific unagi+teriyaki chicken variant code exists. Used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for unagi teriyaki chicken bento identified in published SNOMED CT hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bento box serving is approximately 350 g, comprising grilled unagi, teriyaki chicken, steamed rice, and side vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Unagi + Teriyaki Chicken Bento — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Unagi+Teriyaki Chicken Bento (cr_id G00010129). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on high-protein, very-low-carbohydrate mixed meat dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for unagi+teriyaki chicken bento. The dish contains only 1.62 g carbohydrate per 100 g (predominantly from teriyaki sauce sugars). GI is estimated at approximately 35 (low) by analogy with high-protein, very-low-carbohydrate grilled meat and fish dishes documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Glycemic load per 350 g serving = (35 × 1.62 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 2.0 g, confirming negligible glycemic impact. The very low carbohydrate content means GI classification has minimal practical significance for this dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 723 mg per 100 g — substantially exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, request minimal teriyaki sauce and avoid pickled vegetable sides to reduce sodium load, and limit portion to no more than 100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (1.62 g/100 g) and sugar (1.47 g/100 g) with high protein (25.73 g/100 g) supports post-meal glucose stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without a large rice portion; the high protein content slows gastric emptying and minimises post-meal glucose excursions, but teriyaki sauce sugar should be monitored and reduced where possible.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (723 mg/100 g) raises cardiovascular risk; cholesterol at 105 mg/100 g is moderate; unagi provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids partially offsetting risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and request reduced teriyaki sauce to lower sodium intake; the omega-3 content from unagi is a positive factor, but the high sodium load is the dominant cardiovascular concern and must be managed.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium at 723 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g RA threshold; high sodium worsens RA-related tissue inflammation and swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish in standard preparation; if consumed, request significantly reduced teriyaki sauce to bring sodium below 400 mg/100 g, and note that unagi's omega-3 content is a beneficial anti-inflammatory factor that does not offset the sodium-driven concern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Unagi and Teriyaki Chicken Bento is a Japanese-style bento box meal popular in Singapore food courts and Japanese casual dining restaurants. It combines two protein components: kabayaki-style grilled freshwater eel (unagi) glazed with a sweet soy-based tare sauce, and teriyaki chicken — boneless chicken grilled and coated with teriyaki sauce made from soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar. The bento is typically served with steamed Japanese short-grain rice, pickled vegetables (tsukemono), and a small salad. The flavour profile is savoury-sweet with a caramelised umami depth from the tare and teriyaki glazes. Texture contrasts include the tender, fatty eel against the firmer, juicy chicken. Nutritionally, the dish is high in protein (25.73 g/100 g) and relatively low in fat (5.96 g/100 g) and carbohydrate (1.62 g/100 g) when assessed on the protein-and-eel components alone, though the full bento with rice substantially increases total carbohydrate. Sodium is high at 723 mg/100 g due to soy-sauce-based glazes, making this dish unsuitable for hypertension and rheumatoid arthritis management without sauce reduction. Cholesterol is moderate at 105 mg/100 g. Unagi contributes omega-3 fatty acids beneficial for cardiovascular and anti-inflammatory health. The dish is not halal-certified in standard preparation due to mirin and sake (alcohol-containing ingredients); halal-certified variants may exist at specific outlets. It is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when consumed without a large rice portion.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010129, confidence 0.95). All primary macros and available micronutrients are taken from this approved primary source without estimation. GI is an analogous estimate due to absence of a direct measurement for this specific dish.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this specific bento combination is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry (G00010129) as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this dish. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is a confirmed zero per the HPB source for this dish composition. GI is an analogous estimate (approximately 35, low) based on the very low carbohydrate content (1.62 g/100 g) and high protein composition, referenced against the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables for comparable high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled meat and fish dishes. Halal status is false in standard preparation as teriyaki sauce typically contains mirin and sake (alcohol-containing ingredients); halal-certified variants may exist at specific outlets. The input record listed food_subgroup as 'Chicken-based dishes'; this has been updated to 'Mixed protein dishes — eel and chicken'. This dish is served in Singapore and therefore requires Singaporean multilingual coverage (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) in addition to the Japanese cuisine requirement. Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases have been added in iteration 3 to satisfy G6 and Q8 requirements.
